title: CLO Command
description: A command sent by the client to a CLO backend. Transport-agnostic: the same object is written to task.py-style files, request.json, or sent over a socket.

id.description: Optional correlation id echoed back in the Result.

code.description: Python source to exec against the CLO API. Required when type == run_code. The CLO *_api modules and the helper functions are in scope; set RESULT = <json-able> to return data. May appear here (top level) or inside params.code — clients that wrap all args in params (e.g. the MCP server's send_command) put it there, and dispatch() accepts both.

params.description: Arguments for a typed op (e.g. snapshot's path), and where run_code's `code` lands when a client wraps args in params.
